title: Ansible Collections
learningObjectives: Understand the Ansible Collection format and namespace structure
Install and use collections from Ansible Galaxy and private hubs
Build a custom collection with modules and roles

theory.content:
What are Collections?

Ansible Collections are a distribution format that packages modules, plugins, roles, and playbooks together. Introduced as a stable feature in Ansible 2.9, collections are the future of content distribution, replacing the monolithic Ansible package.

Collection Namespace

Collections use a namespace.collection naming convention. For example, community.general or amazon.aws. Modules within are referenced as namespace.collection.module.

Installing Collections

# Install from Galaxy
ansible-galaxy collection install community.general

# Install specific version
ansible-galaxy collection install amazon.aws:==6.0.0

# Install from requirements.yml
ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml

requirements.yml for Collections

collections:
  - name: community.general
    version: '>=7.0.0'
  - name: amazon.aws
    version: '6.0.0'
  - name: my_namespace.my_collection
    source: https://my.automation.hub/
    type: galaxy

Collection Directory Structure

my_namespace/my_collection/
  galaxy.yml           # Collection metadata
  README.md
  plugins/
    modules/
      my_module.py
    inventory/
    lookup/
  roles/
    my_role/
  playbooks/
  docs/
  tests/

Building and Publishing

# Build a collection tarball
ansible-galaxy collection build

# Publish to Galaxy
ansible-galaxy collection publish my_namespace-my_collection-1.0.0.tar.gz \
  --api-key=YOUR_TOKEN

Using Collection Content

---
- name: Use community.general collection
  hosts: all
  tasks:
    - name: Manage systemd service
      community.general.supervisorctl:
        name: myapp
        state: started

    - name: Use lookup plugin from collection
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg: "{{ lookup('community.general.random_string', length=16) }}"
